examples.title: Vector Addition using CUDA
examples.description: Simple CUDA kernel demonstrating parallel array addition where each thread handles one element
examples.explanation: Each thread calculates its global index and performs one element addition. Threads are organized into blocks (256 threads each), blocks into grid. Simple demonstration of data-parallel execution.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) total work, O(n/T) per thread where T = total threads
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) for input/output arrays plus O(1) per thread for registers

examples.title: Matrix Multiplication with Shared Memory
examples.description: Optimized matrix multiplication using shared memory for tile-based computation
examples.explanation: Uses shared memory to load matrix tiles, reducing global memory accesses. Each block computes a TILE_SIZE x TILE_SIZE output block. __syncthreads() ensures proper synchronization within block.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(T²) shared memory per block + O(M*N + M*K + K*N) global memory

examples.title: Multicore Parallel Reduction
examples.description: Parallel sum reduction across multiple CPU cores using OpenMP
examples.explanation: Parallel reduction divides array among threads, each computes partial sum, then combines. OpenMP handles thread creation, work distribution, and reduction. Efficient for large arrays on multicore CPUs.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n/p + log p) where p = number of cores
examples.spaceComplexity: O(p) for thread-local storage + O(1) per thread for accumulation
